title: H_001 LPP source-power ceiling — representative LPP budget (~250 W class → ~1.5 kW pushed) falls ~2 kW short of the ~3.5 kW the 6.5 nm optics demand (H_002 ×14) requires. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — baseline 250 W, ceiling 1500 W, demand 3548 W (gap 2048 W). LPP power-scaling alone insufficient for 6.5 nm on this representative budget; engineering-horizon wall (reopenable). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_002 BEUV (6.5 nm) mirror-throughput wall — same 11-mirror column passes ~14x fewer photons at 6.5 nm (La/B R=0.55) than 13.5 nm (Mo/Si R=0.70), forcing ~14x more source power. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — power_multiplier=14.19x (T_euv=0.0198, T_beuv=0.00139; N=7→5.41x), 5/5 falsifiers PASS. Optics-side source wall before emitter/resist penalties.

title: H_003 NA vs depth-of-focus trade — NA 0.33→0.55 prints 1.67x finer but loses 2.78x focus depth; DoF penalty (NA²) strictly steeper than resolution gain (NA¹). SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — res 12.27→7.36 nm (1.667x), DoF 61.98→22.31 nm (2.778x = (NA_hi/NA_lo)²). Geometric root of the ultra-thin-resist wall. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_004 compact plasma-wakefield accelerator as light-source enabler — Dawson E_0=96.16 GV/m (~3200x RF) reaches 1 GeV in ~1 cm vs ~33 m. Reference-matched to demiurge cern-accelerator. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — E_0=96.15920 GV/m (|Δ|=0 vs demiurge ref), gradient 3205x RF, length 3205x shorter. Compactness enabler for a tabletop EUV source; photon-generation downstream (L2). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_005 HVM throughput floor — HVM needs ≥100 WPH but 6.5 nm at the same source manages only ~11 WPH (1/14 photons, H_002), an order of magnitude below the floor → throughput/economics wall. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — 13.5nm 150 WPH vs 6.5nm 10.6 WPH at same source (floor 100); restoring floor needs ~2366 W (beyond H_001 LPP ceiling). Throughput wall from transcript 100-WPH threshold. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_006 accelerator → EUV photons — undulator (15mm,K=1) makes 13.5nm at ~0.47 GeV (single-stage LPA reach); inverse-Compton (1µm) at ~2 MeV. Closes H_004 e-beam→light gap. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — undulator 13.5nm@466.5 MeV (≤1 GeV), inverse-Compton 13.5nm@2.20 MeV; both roundtrip-exact. Accelerator path makes light, not just electrons (fixes H_004 L2). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_007 sub-13.5 nm tunability by electron energy — same undulator spans 13.5/6.5/5/3 nm at 466/672/766/990 MeV (~2.1× dial, all ≤1 GeV). Shorter λ = a knob, not an emitter+multilayer swap. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — 13.5→3nm = 466.5→989.5 MeV (2.12× dial, γ~1/√λ), all within single-stage LPA reach. Accelerator source sidesteps the LPP emitter(Sn→Gd/Lu/La)+multilayer(Mo/Si→La/B) double-swap. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_008 accelerator-source flux wall — at present LPA rep rates the undulator avg in-band EUV power is ~10³–10⁵× below the ~167 W for 100 WPH; compact path is flux/average-power limited, not wavelength-limited. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — HVM needs ~167 W; LPA now ~1 mW (gap 1.7e5×), pushed 100 kHz ~0.1 W (gap 1667×). Real wall is rep-rate/brightness (reopenable horizon), not wavelength. Honest counterweight to H_004/006/007. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_009 beam energy spread → undulator linewidth — ~1% LPA energy spread gives ~2% Δλ/λ, total 2.24% > 2% in-band budget, swamps higher harmonics → erodes in-band flux (compounds H_008). SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — n=1 total 2.24% (natural 1% ⊕ spread 2%) > 2% budget; n=3 natural 0.33% << spread 2%. Beam quality limits in-band fraction (reopenable: staged LPA <0.1%). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_010 synchrotron vs compact LPA — storage ring meets HVM power but ~4 EUV-tools cost + ~200× footprint; LPA inverts it (tiny footprint, flux wall). Accelerator landscape = footprint↔throughput trade. SUPPORTED 4/4.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — footprint 200×, cost 4.3 EUV-tools, synchrotron meets 167 W / LPA doesn't. Neither corner free; design picks which wall to break (shrink ring vs raise LPA flux). 4/4 PASS.

title: H_011 accelerator wavelength reach — no theoretical short-λ floor; λ~1/E² walks EUV→water-window→X-ray. Single-stage LPA (≤1 GeV)→~3nm, few-stage (≤2 GeV)→~1nm. Wall is flux/beam-quality, not wavelength. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — 0.5/1/2/5/10 GeV → 11.75/2.94/0.73/0.12/0.03 nm; 3nm@0.99 GeV, 1nm@1.71 GeV, 0.1nm@5.42 GeV; λ∝1/E² exact. No wavelength floor — real cap is flux(H_008)/beam-quality(H_009). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_A1 cavity-enhanced inverse-Compton + attosecond timing lock — decouple EUV photon rep-rate from the laser amplifier + lock linewidth → break flux+beam-quality wall from a sub-cm accelerator. Prediction: enhancement≥10⁴ & jitter≤1fs → ≥167W in-band, Δλ/λ≤1%.
verdict: none — 🜂 abstract conjecture, unverified (no run)

title: H_A2 compact LPA-driven micro-SASE FEL — exponential photon gain ~100×/bunch at the flux-wall root, tabletop. Prediction: SASE saturation ≤5m undulator. Threatened by H_009 energy spread.
verdict: none — 🜂 abstract conjecture, unverified (no run)

title: H_A3 recirculating micro-storage + cryo many-period undulator — multi-turn (×N flux) + N≥100 cold periods (narrow line) break flux AND beam-quality together. Prediction: ≥20 turns & N≥100 → in-band ×30, Δλ/λ≤1%.
verdict: none — 🜂 abstract conjecture, unverified (no run)

title: H_A4 shot-by-shot spectral gating + grazing-incidence column — recover in-band flux from imperfect source + cut optics loss; cheapest near-term lever. Prediction: wafer in-band watts ≥5× ungated normal-incidence baseline. Runnable next.
verdict: promoted — runnable core verified as H_012 (🟢 8.03× recovery, 5/5)

title: H_A5 all-optical EUV (HHG→plasma-mirror amplify→metasurface relay) — delete the accelerator entirely; accelerator-free moonshot. Prediction: end-to-end ≥10W in-band 13.5nm (HVM-candidacy threshold, ~10⁴× over HHG records).
verdict: none — 🜂 abstract conjecture, unverified (most speculative)

title: H_012 spectral gating + grazing-incidence in-band recovery — 4/11 surfaces grazing (R=0.9) + gating lifts wafer in-band flux ~8× over normal-incidence ungated at 6.5 nm (≥5× bar). Promotes abstract H_A4. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — recovery 8.03× (baseline 9.75e-4 → enhanced 7.83e-3); cheapest, least-exotic flux-wall lever. Recovery not generation (flux H_008 untouched). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_013 energy-spread cooling splits the M4 brightness wall — cooling σγ/γ 1.0→0.85% pushes the line 2.24→1.97% (inside 2% budget: quality wall BEATEN) while average power unchanged (flux wall STUBBORN). From abstraction-lane prediction P4. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — quality sub-wall beatable by cooling (non-conservative, Liouville-exempt), flux sub-wall (H_008) terminal. Fleet loop: meta-law M4 → escape E4 → prediction P4 → verified. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_B1 no-SPF narrow-line recovery — intrinsically narrow source deletes ~25–30% spectral-purity-filter loss + doubles as actinic inspection. Cheapest near-term in-band recovery. Prediction: ≥25% delivered in-band recovered.
verdict: promoted — runnable core verified as H_014 (🟢 1.43× recovery, 5/5)

title: H_B2 high-cross-section ultrathin resist — 4–6× EUV absorption in ≤12 nm MOx/metamaterial film multiplies effective dose (demand side). Prediction: ≥2× effective dose at equal LER.
verdict: none — 🜂 abstract conjecture, unverified (RLS trade is the risk)

title: H_B3 two-color narrow-arm litho — EUV only arms, cheap flood fires → EUV photon demand ≤15–20%. Prediction: node CD at ≤20% single-color EUV dose.
verdict: none — 🜂 abstract conjecture, unverified (armed-state blur is the risk)

title: H_B4 re-invertible high-rep recombination EUV laser — only intrinsically-narrow, undulator-free source-side escape from the flux wall (sidesteps accelerator, meta-law M2/E2). Prediction: gL≥14 at ≥10 kHz → ≥10 mW in-band, scaling path to 167 W.
verdict: partially promoted — geometry/requirement verified as H_017; 13.5nm gain remains the open frontier

title: H_B5 digital photon-counting litho — Geiger-mode resist + photon-counting metrology converts dose-to-clear into a counting problem. Prediction: print at ⟨5 photons/pixel⟩, <1 ppm error, if per-photon gain >10³.
verdict: none — 🜂 abstract conjecture, unverified (lateral blur vs gain is the crux)

title: H_014 no-SPF in-band recovery — narrow source drops the spectral-purity filter a broadband LPP needs → recovers ~43% in-band power; narrow required (broadband exceeds OOB tolerance). Promotes abstract H_B1. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — recovery 1.43× (drop 0.70-transmission SPF); narrow can skip SPF, broadband can't. Recovery not generation (H_008 untouched), conditional on H_B4. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_015 wall census — across 6 sub-13.5nm architectures the binding wall is always one of {M1 photon, M2 power, M3 footprint, M4 flux}, NEVER wavelength (M5 confirmed); accelerator routes bind on M4 (flux). SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — LPP→M2, synchrotron→M3, LPA-undulator/inverse-Compton→M4, recomb-laser→M2, BEUV-optics→M1; 0 bind on wavelength. Flux (M4) is the accelerator-route terminal wall. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_016 ERL reopens the flux floor — energy recovery lifts rep-rate by 1/(1-η) with NO thermodynamic cap; ERL+FEL reaches 200W (>167 floor) compact, ERL alone 2W (product wall). Flux is engineering-reopenable, not a 2nd-law ceiling. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — rep-rate ceiling 1e5→1e6 (η0.9, 10×)→diverges (η→1); ERL+FEL 200W ≥167, ERL-alone 2W insufficient (necessary not sufficient). Flux REOPENABLE — terminal question now engineering/economic (compact 167W at affordable wall-plug). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_017 recombination EUV laser saturation requirement — plasma waveguide makes gL≥14 reachable at modest ~1 cm⁻¹ gain (reference-matched to 46.9 nm Ar laser); the 13.5 nm gain is the open frontier, not geometry. Promotes H_B4. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — 46.9nm Ar gL=14 anchor, waveguide 140× length, required 13.5nm gain 1.0 cm⁻¹ (modest). Geometry reachable; 13.5nm gain unverified (honest frontier). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_018 ERL beam-breakup is not the binding wall — ERL+FEL EUV needs ~0.2 mA, ~100× below HOM-damped BBU threshold (~20 mA); binding ERL limit is heat/wall-plug, not instability. SUPPORTED 4/4.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — needed 0.2 mA vs BBU 20 mA (100× margin); HOM damping 20× lever. Localizes terminal wall to heat/wall-plug+economics (H_016). 4/4 PASS.

title: H_019 cost-of-ownership conjunction — compact coherent/ERL beats LPP $/wafer-layer ONLY when η≥~4× (M6) AND amortized across M≥~3 scanners (M9); neither alone wins; M8 waste-heat OPEX floor never vanishes. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — both-levers 0.55 WIN vs η-only 1.35 / amort-only 1.00 (both LOSE); M→∞ asymptotes to M8 floor 0.15 (never 0). Economics reopenable down to the M8 waste-heat asymptote. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_020 real-units cost-of-ownership — with public-order figures the energy+waste-heat (M8) term is ~0.5% of LPP cost-of-ownership; binding economic wall is CAPEX+maintenance (M7/M9), NOT the M8 thermodynamic floor. Settles the campaign's last open unknown. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — CAPEX/yr $70M + maint $87.5M vs energy $0.77M → M8=0.48% of CoO, CAPEX+maint=99.5%. M8 floor is real but ~200× below LPP all-in cost: NOT the decision wall, CAPEX is. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_021 tabletop accelerator wins the CAPEX wall via the M7 learning curve — replicable modules cross below LPP tool CAPEX at ~6 units (Wright LR 0.85); monolithic synchrotron stuck at 4× (no replication). Revives the compact route. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — compact CAPEX N=1 1.50→N=10 0.87→N=100 0.51, crossover ~5.6 modules; synchrotron 4.0 never rides the curve. The small replicable tabletop accelerator wins the CAPEX terminal wall. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_022 combination breakthrough — tabletop accelerator + ERL + FEL + cooling + module-array clears flux (200W) AND CAPEX (0.87<LPP) AND wavelength tunability simultaneously; each lever necessary. Realizes goal 'tabletop accelerator + @ combination'. SUPPORTED 6/6.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — stack 200W≥167 (flux), 0.87<1.0 (CAPEX), tunable (wavelength) all cleared; drop FEL→2W or array→1.5 reopens a wall (each necessary). Compact-accelerator combination breaks every campaign wall at once. 6/6 PASS.

title: H_023 ERL↔FEL cross-coupling holds but is the combination's tightest link — cooled 0.85% inside FEL Pierce ρ~1% (margin 1.18×), post-FEL 1.31% inside ERL acceptance 2% (margin 1.52×); FEL beam-quality is the binding coupling. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — both couplings consistent; FEL beam-quality margin 1.18× is the binding cross-coupling (ρ-conditional, L1). Deeper cooling buys margin; source-coherent @-routes sidestep it. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_024 cooling is the clean lever, raising FEL Pierce ρ is a trap — deeper cooling lifts BOTH FEL+ERL margins; raising ρ widens FEL but crushes ERL acceptance <1 (recovery fails) past ρ≈1.8%. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — σ0.5% → FEL 2.0×/ERL 1.79× (both up); ρ2% → FEL 2.35×/ERL 0.92× (ERL fails), crossover ρ=1.81%. The tightest link (H_023) opens by cooling, not ρ. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_025 @-family depletion census — @-combination space mapped & dry: of 4 families exactly 1 clears all walls (accelerator-coherent spine H_022), 3 fall on distinct walls, supply(3)+demand(1) taxonomy complete → DEPLETION. Closes the goal. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — 1 clearer + 3 orthogonal partials (flux/rls/capex-redundant) + complete taxonomy → @-space dry. Goal 'breakthrough + deepen-to-depletion' met; binding work now CAPEX/M7 + cooling stage, not @-search. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_026 sourced re-verification — published data CONFIRMS core inputs (optics R, LPP 205-250W, ~0.02% wall-plug, $200-400M CAPEX) and CORRECTS 3 optimistic links (La/B 0.58→wall ~8×; demonstrated 46.9nm gL~8.3<14; raw LPA spread several%). SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — core REAL not fabricated; 3 corrections all make engineering harder not easier; physics-wall conclusions hold. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_027 break integration wall (weakness 1) — 3 lenses: every subsystem standalone-demonstrated (min TRL 4>1), synchrotron/FEL integration precedent, start-to-end sim → bounded-engineering wall, not invention. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — integration is a TRL/engineering gap (combination TRL~2-3), reopenable; not new physics. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_028 break FEL thin-margin wall (weakness 2) — 3 independent escapes: cooling→2.0×, inverse-Compton sidesteps σ<ρ entirely, larger ERL acceptance pushes ρ-trap crossover 1.81%→2.88%. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — thin FEL margin reopenable by 3 independent escapes (widen/remove/relax), not a single fragile path. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_029 break replicable-module wall (weakness 3) — 3 lenses: ~70% standard mass-produced parts, only ~6 units to beat LPP, fiber-combining drive. Replication risk concentrates on ~30% bespoke. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — the leap of faith is bounded to a minority-of-cost low-volume fiber-based problem (bespoke 30% = the hard part). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_030 funded-reality reference-match — accelerator-FEL EUV is real+funded (xLight $40M+CHIPS, KEK 10kW) validating the direction, BUT funded driver = conventional ERL not tabletop LPA, funded leg = M9-amortization not M7-module-array, 13.5nm-on-LPA + single-pass cooling undemonstrated, concept predates lumen (Nakajima 2014). SUPPORTED 6/6.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — direction validated by industry money; 3 honest corrections (driver/econ-leg/wavelength+cooling) + not novel. Robust restatement is xLight-shaped (shared ERL amortized across scanners), not tabletop-LPA-shaped. 6/6 PASS.

title: H_031 fleet-r2 — binding FEL constraint is SLICE spread not undulator length (dechirp≠cooling; 0.5% slice ~5× over Ming-Xie → no 13.5nm lasing, floor ~24.8nm, TGU=named unverified escape); H_021 FEL<LPP holds only at fan-out N≥2 (~8× @N=10, inverts @N=1), cryomodule Wright shallow 0.95 → M9 not M7. SUPPORTED 6/6.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — wavelength dial bounded by slice spread (cooling undemonstrated, dechirp insufficient); CAPEX win real only amortized (M9). 6/6 PASS.

title: H_032 TGU escape classified — the last orthogonal escape (transverse-gradient undulator) is insufficient for LPA (never lased anywhere; dispersion breaks LPA emittance past 13.5nm matching; LWFA-FEL floor 275nm), so the slice-spread wall STANDS for tabletop-LPA but is CLEARED by the funded conventional ERL (FLASH 13nm 2006) = architecture wall not physics ceiling. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — TGU can't rescue LPA at 13.5nm; answer is the shared conventional-ERL FEL, not the tabletop LPA. Campaign converged. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_033 BREAKTHROUGH — the slice-spread wall is FEL-gain-specific; inverse-Compton (ICS) is the orthogonal gain-free mechanism immune to it, reaching 13.5nm with only ~2.2 MeV (~455× less than an undulator FEL), slice spread only broadens the line to ~1% (within 2% passband). Compact 13.5nm reopens; wall relocates to flux (reopenable H_016). SUPPORTED 6/6.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — slice-spread wall broken for the compact-source question via ICS (no gain condition, ~2 MeV, tabletop); honest residual = the relocated flux wall, undemonstrated at litho power. 6/6 PASS.

title: H_034 deepen — the ICS residual flux wall (~7e5× short of 100W litho target) is reopenable in principle by recirculation (optical cavity 1e4 × ERL 1e3 = 1e7 > gap, ~15× margin), same family as H_016; honest residual: litho-power ICS-EUV undemonstrated, needs near-maximal cavity AND ERL together. SUPPORTED 6/6.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — ICS flux wall classified reopenable (recirc 1e7 > 7e5 gap) but thin 15× margin + undemonstrated at EUV; simultaneity is the next front. 6/6 PASS.

title: H_035 cycle-2 breakthrough — the ICS enhancement cavity is at the IR DRIVE wavelength (finesse 1e4-1e5 routine), not at EUV (R~0.70 -> finesse ~9, forbidden); so the H_034 flux-recirculation gain rests on demonstrated IR-cavity tech (margin robust). Honest residual: integrated high-avg-power EUV-ICS undemonstrated. SUPPORTED 6/6.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — 'EUV cavity impossible' is a misclassification; cavity is at IR. Margin robust. 6/6 PASS.

tier: 🟢 SUPPORTED (numerical · loop cycle-2 심화 · honest negative)
title: H_036 cycle-2 deepen (honest negative) — the ICS flux margin does NOT survive bandwidth-collection derating (~50x eats the H_034 15x margin -> 0.3x LOST); recoverable only by stacking demonstrated headroom (finesse x10 AND ERL current x10 = x100 -> ~30x), both-maxed-at-EUV undemonstrated. Compact-ICS is possible but margin-thin. SUPPORTED 6/6.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — bandwidth-limited collection eats the margin; reopens only by stacked maxima -> margin-thin, reinforces conventional-ERL as the funded answer. 6/6 PASS.
